title: Fermented Gooey Ukrainian Beef Stew
description: A midnight-craving Ukrainian stew that marries hearty beef with fermented flavors, mustard-infused deglaze, and a silky, paleo-friendly sauce.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the beef cubes and sear until browned on all sides, then remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium, add the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, and sauté the onions until translucent about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Sprinkle in the mustard seeds and ground mustard powder, stirring to toast them lightly for 30 seconds.
Pour in the fermented mustard sauce and deglaze the pan, scraping up all browned bits from the bottom.
Return the seared beef to the pot, add the fermented cabbage juice, tomato paste, bay leaf, and beef broth. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, cover, and let cook for 60 minutes, then uncover and continue simmering for an additional 30 minutes, or until the beef is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, and adjust thickness with a splash of water if needed.
Transfer the stew to serving bowls, sprinkle with fresh dill, and serve hot with a side of paleo-friendly bread if desired.
